text:
AgamiSoft Ltd.
Company Profile
1. About AgamiSoft
AgamiSoft Ltd. is an enterprise software company founded in 2015 and headquartered in Dhaka,
Bangladesh. The company designs and delivers AI-powered business applications for clients in
banking, retail, logistics, and healthcare. AgamiSoft employs approximately 240 people across three
offices and serves customers in 12 countries.
2. Mission and Vision
Our mission is to make enterprise software intelligent, accessible, and dependable. Our vision is to
become the leading provider of applied AI solutions in South Asia by 2030.
3. Core Values
 Customer First: We measure success by the outcomes our clients achieve.
 Engineering Excellence: We favor simple, well-tested, maintainable systems.
 Integrity: We are transparent with clients, colleagues, and partners.
 Continuous Learning: We invest in the growth of every employee.

ble systems.
 Integrity: We are transparent with clients, colleagues, and partners.
 Continuous Learning: We invest in the growth of every employee.
4. Products and Services
AgamiSoft offers three primary product lines and a professional services practice.
Offering
Description
AgamiFlow
Workflow automation platform for back-office operations.
AgamiInsight
Analytics and reporting suite with natural-language querying.
AgamiAssist
Embeddable AI assistant framework for enterprise apps.
Professional Services
Custom development, integration, and AI consulting.
5. Leadership
The company is led by Chief Executive Officer Msaid Rahman and Chief Technology Officer Nabila
Haque. The engineering organization is divided into Platform, Applied AI, and Client Delivery groups.
6. Contact
Head Office: Level 7, Software Technology Park, Kawran Bazar, Dhaka 1215, Bangladesh. General

form, Applied AI, and Client Delivery groups.
6. Contact
Head Office: Level 7, Software Technology Park, Kawran Bazar, Dhaka 1215, Bangladesh. General
enquiries: info@agamisoft.example. Support: support@agamisoft.example. Phone: +880 2 5566 7788.

AgamiSoft Ltd.
Employee Handbook
1. Welcome
Welcome to AgamiSoft Ltd. This handbook introduces you to how we work, the benefits available to
you, and the everyday practices that keep the company running smoothly. It complements the HR
Policy and Leave Policy, which take precedence where any conflict exists.
2. Onboarding
New employees complete onboarding within their first week. This includes IT account setup, a security
and confidentiality briefing, an introduction to the team, and assignment of a workplace buddy for the
first 30 days.
3. Benefits
 Health Insurance: Group medical coverage for the employee, spouse, and up to two children.
 Provident Fund: The company contributes 10% of basic salary, matched by the employee.
 Annual Bonus: Two festival bonuses per year, each one month of basic salary.
 Learning Budget: Up to BDT 40,000 per year for courses, books, and conferences.

onus: Two festival bonuses per year, each one month of basic salary.
 Learning Budget: Up to BDT 40,000 per year for courses, books, and conferences.
 Wellness: Subsidized gym membership and an annual health check-up.
4. Equipment and IT
Each employee receives a laptop and the software needed for their role. Equipment remains company
property and must be returned upon exit. Report lost or stolen devices to IT immediately at
it-help@agamisoft.example.
5. Expense Reimbursement
Business expenses are reimbursed when supported by valid receipts and submitted through the finance
portal within 30 days. Travel, client meals, and approved training costs are eligible. Reimbursements
are paid with the next monthly payroll.
6. Communication Tools
The company uses email for formal communication, a chat platform for day-to-day collaboration, and a

the next monthly payroll.
6. Communication Tools
The company uses email for formal communication, a chat platform for day-to-day collaboration, and a
project tracker for task management. Employees should check email at least once each working day.
7. Information Security
 Use strong, unique passwords and enable multi-factor authentication.
 Never share client data outside approved systems.

 Lock your screen when away from your desk.
 Report suspected security incidents to security@agamisoft.example within 24 hours.
8. Dress Code
The everyday dress code is smart casual. Business formal attire is expected for client meetings and
official company events.
9. Grievance Procedure
Employees who wish to raise a concern should first speak with their direct manager. If unresolved, the
concern may be escalated to HR in writing. HR aims to respond within five working days.
10. Exit Process
On departure, employees complete a handover, return company equipment, and attend an exit interview
with HR. Access to company systems is revoked on the last working day, and final settlement follows
within 30 days.

AgamiSoft Ltd.
Frequently Asked Questions
This document answers common questions from employees and clients. For full details, refer to the
relevant policy or handbook.
HR and Employment
Q: What are the standard working hours?
A: Standard hours are 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM, Sunday to Thursday, with a one-hour lunch break, totaling
a 40-hour week. See the HR Policy for details.
Q: How long is the probation period?
A: Probation lasts six months. During this time either party may end the employment with two weeks'
notice.
Q: How much notice must I give to resign?
A: Confirmed employees give 30 days' written notice; employees on probation give two weeks' notice.
Leave
Q: How many annual leave days do I get?
A: Confirmed full-time employees receive 20 earned leave days per year, plus 10 casual and 14 sick
leave days. See the Leave Policy.
Q: Can I carry unused leave into next year?

loyees receive 20 earned leave days per year, plus 10 casual and 14 sick
leave days. See the Leave Policy.
Q: Can I carry unused leave into next year?
A: Up to 10 unused earned leave days may carry over and must be used by 31 March. Casual and sick
leave cannot be carried over.
Q: How do I apply for leave?
A: Submit requests through the HR portal at least three working days in advance for planned leave.
Sick or casual leave may be reported to your manager the same day.
Benefits
Q: What is the learning budget?
A: Each employee may claim up to BDT 40,000 per year for courses, books, and conferences.
Q: Does the company offer a provident fund?
A: Yes. The company contributes 10% of basic salary, matched by the employee.
Sales and Pricing

Q: What discount can an Account Executive approve alone?
A: Up to 10%. Discounts of 11-20% need Sales Manager approval, and above 20% need Head of Sales
approval. No discount may exceed 35%.
Q: What is the quarterly sales quota?
A: Each Account Executive carries a quota of $150,000 in new annual recurring revenue per quarter.
IT and Security
Q: Who do I contact if my laptop is lost or stolen?
A: Report it to IT immediately at it-help@agamisoft.example, and report any security incident to
security@agamisoft.example within 24 hours.
Q: Can I work remotely?
A: Employees may work remotely up to two days per week with manager approval. Fully remote
arrangements require Head of Department and HR approval.

AgamiSoft Ltd.
HR Policy
1. Purpose and Scope
This HR Policy governs the employment relationship between AgamiSoft Ltd. and its employees. It
applies to all full-time, part-time, and probationary employees unless a written contract states
otherwise.
2. Working Hours
Standard working hours are 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM, Sunday through Thursday, including a one-hour
lunch break. The standard work week is 40 hours. Employees are expected to be available during core
hours of 11:00 AM to 4:00 PM.
3. Remote and Hybrid Work
Employees may work remotely up to two days per week with manager approval. Fully remote
arrangements require Head of Department and HR approval and are reviewed every six months.
4. Probation Period
New employees serve a probation period of six months. During probation, either party may terminate

are reviewed every six months.
4. Probation Period
New employees serve a probation period of six months. During probation, either party may terminate
the employment with two weeks' written notice. Confirmation of employment follows a satisfactory
performance review.
5. Compensation and Payroll
Salaries are paid monthly on the last working day of each month by bank transfer. Annual salary
reviews take place in January. Employees are entitled to two festival bonuses per year, each equal to
one month's basic salary.
6. Code of Conduct
 Treat colleagues, clients, and partners with respect and professionalism.
 Protect confidential company and client information at all times.
 Avoid conflicts of interest and disclose any that arise to HR.
 Comply with all applicable laws and company security policies.
7. Anti-Harassment

.
 Avoid conflicts of interest and disclose any that arise to HR.
 Comply with all applicable laws and company security policies.
7. Anti-Harassment
AgamiSoft maintains a zero-tolerance policy toward harassment or discrimination of any kind.
Complaints may be raised confidentially to the HR department and will be investigated within ten
working days.

8. Performance Reviews
Formal performance reviews are conducted twice a year, in June and December. Reviews assess goal
achievement, competencies, and development needs. Ratings range from 1 (Needs Improvement) to 5
(Outstanding).
9. Disciplinary Procedure
The disciplinary process follows four stages: verbal warning, written warning, final written warning,
and termination. Serious misconduct may result in immediate dismissal without prior warnings.
10. Resignation and Notice
Confirmed employees must provide 30 days' written notice of resignation. Employees on probation
must provide two weeks' notice. The final settlement is processed within 30 days of the last working
day.

AgamiSoft Ltd.
Leave Policy
1. Overview
This Leave Policy describes the types of leave available to employees of AgamiSoft Ltd., the
entitlements for each, and the procedure for requesting leave. The leave year runs from 1 January to 31
December.
2. Leave Entitlements
Annual entitlements for confirmed full-time employees are summarized below.
Leave Type
Days per Year
Notes
Annual (Earned) Leave
20
Accrues monthly; up to 10 days may carry over.
Casual Leave
10
For short-notice personal matters.
Sick Leave
14
Medical certificate required beyond 2 consecutive days.
Maternity Leave
112 (16 weeks)
Fully paid; up to two occasions.
Paternity Leave
10
Within 30 days of childbirth.
Bereavement Leave
5
For an immediate family member.
3. Public Holidays
Public holidays are additional to the entitlements above and follow the government-declared holiday

For an immediate family member.
3. Public Holidays
Public holidays are additional to the entitlements above and follow the government-declared holiday
calendar, typically 16 to 22 days per year. If a public holiday falls on a weekly holiday, no substitute
day is granted.
4. Applying for Leave
Leave requests must be submitted through the HR portal at least three working days in advance for
planned leave. Sick and casual leave may be reported to the direct manager on the same day. All leave
is subject to manager approval based on operational needs.
5. Carry-Over and Encashment
A maximum of 10 unused annual leave days may be carried into the next leave year and must be used
by 31 March. Unused casual and sick leave cannot be carried over or encashed. On resignation, unused
earned leave is encashed at the basic salary rate.
6. Unpaid Leave

ed casual and sick leave cannot be carried over or encashed. On resignation, unused
earned leave is encashed at the basic salary rate.
6. Unpaid Leave
Where an employee has exhausted all paid leave, unpaid leave may be granted at management
discretion for up to 30 days per year. Unpaid leave beyond 30 days requires Head of Department and

HR approval.
7. Leave During Probation
Employees on probation accrue leave but may only take sick and casual leave. Earned leave taken
during probation requires special approval from HR.

AgamiSoft Ltd.
Sales Handbook
1. Purpose
This Sales Handbook guides the AgamiSoft sales team in engaging prospects, managing opportunities,
and closing deals consistently. It defines our process, pricing structure, and approval rules.
2. Sales Process
The AgamiSoft sales pipeline has five stages.
Stage
Description
Owner
1. Lead
Inbound or outbound prospect identified.

2. Qualified

3. Proposal
Solution and pricing presented to the client.
Account Executive
4. Negotiation
Terms, scope, and contract finalized.

5. Closed
Contract signed and handed to Delivery.

4. Discount Approval
Discounts must be approved according to their size. Account Executives may approve up to 10%
independently. Discounts from 11% to 20% require Sales Manager approval. Discounts above 20%
require Head of Sales approval. No discount may exceed 35% under any circumstances.
5. Sales Targets and Commission
Each Account Executive carries a quarterly quota of $150,000 in new annual recurring revenue.
Commission is 8% of closed ARR, rising to 12% on revenue above quota. Commission is paid in the
month following contract signature.
6. Proposals and Contracts

All proposals must use the approved template and be reviewed by a Sales Manager before being sent.
Contracts require legal review when the term exceeds 12 months or the annual value exceeds $50,000.
7. CRM Hygiene
 Log every client interaction in the CRM within 24 hours.
 Keep opportunity stages and close dates accurate.
 Attach signed contracts to the opportunity record before handover.
8. Handover to Delivery
Within three working days of closing, the Account Executive holds a handover meeting with the
Delivery team, sharing the signed contract, scope, key contacts, and any commitments made during the
sale.
